'''Paul Medley''' (born 21 September 1966) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], and at club level for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]]/[[Bradford Bulls]] and [[Dewsbury Rams]], as a {{rlp|sr}}, or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
===Leeds===
Medley signed for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] from the club's Colts team in 1984. He made his first team debut in January 1985 against [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web |title=1988 Elland Road memories return |url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2018/03/13/1988-elland-road-memories-return/ |website=Leeds Rhinos |access-date=7 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221108113423/https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2018/03/13/1988-elland-road-memories-return/ |archive-date=8 November 2022 |date=13 March 2018}}</ref>

Medley played at {{rlp|sr}} in [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]' 14–15 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1987–88 League Cup (rugby league)|1987–88 John Player Special Trophy]] Final during the [[1987–88 Rugby Football League season|1987–88 season]] at [[Central Park (Wigan)|Central Park]], [[Wigan]] on Saturday 9 January 1988.

Medley appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|sr}} [[Mark Brooke-Cowden]]) and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in Leeds' 33–12 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1988 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1988–89 Rugby Football League season|1988–89 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Sunday 16 October 1988.

In January 1989, Medley was sold to [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]] as part of a exchange deal for [[Paul Dixon (rugby league)|Paul Dixon]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Dixon for Leeds in £140,000 deal |url=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/bl/0000901/19890105/020/0020 |work=Huddersfield Daily Examiner |date=5 January 1989 |page=20 |via=[[British Newspaper Archive]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref>

===Bradford===
Following Halifax's relegation from the First Division at the end of the 1988–89 season, Medley was signed by [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] for a club record fee of £110,000.<ref>{{cite news |title=Medley moves |url=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/bl/0003600/19890808/031/0031 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=8 August 1989 |page=31 |via=[[British Newspaper Archive]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref>

Medley appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|pr}} [[Jon Hamer]]) in Bradford Northern's 20–14 victory over [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[1989 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season|1989–90 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Sunday 5 November 1989.

Medley played {{rlp|sr}} in [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]]'s 2–12 defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1990–91 League Cup (rugby league)|1990–91 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 12 January 1991.

Medley played {{rlp|sr}} in 8–15 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992–93 League Cup (rugby league)|1992–93 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 23 January 1993.<ref name="23rd January 1993: Bradford 8 Wigan 15 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3004|title=23rd January 1993: Bradford 8 Wigan 15 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180619140552/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3004|archive-date=19 June 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He appeared for Bradford Bulls from the bench in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final defeat by St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|access-date=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He played 385 games and scored 149 tries in a career that lasted 15 years. He was one of the key players of the Bradford Bulls for 10 years.

Medley played one final season with [[Dewsbury Rams]], helping them reach the [[1999 Northern Ford Premiership]] Grand Final before announcing his retirement.<ref>{{cite web |title=Medley calls it a day after a 15-year career |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/8062030.medley-calls-it-a-day-after-a-15-year-career/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=7 April 2023 |date=2 November 1999}}</ref>

===Representative career===
Medley was selected for the [[1988 Great Britain Lions tour]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Post-playing career==
After retiring as a player, Medley returned to [[Bradford Bulls]], and worked in a number of different roles at the club, including player performance manager and being head of the club's youth academy. He left the club in 2014 to become the national player development manager for the [[Rugby Football League]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Paul Medley to leave Bradford Bulls for RFL player development role |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/11108776.paul-medley-to-leave-bradford-bulls-for-rfl-player-development-role/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=7 April 2023 |date=27 March 2014}}</ref>
